package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for route_param_name_and_dto_field_name_drift_without_mapping_helper
// Anti-pattern: Route param names and DTO field names drift apart with no mapping helper.
type UpdateUserDTO struct {
	UserID   string `json:"user_id"`
	UserName string `json:"user_name"`
	Email    string `json:"email"`
}

func HandleUpdateUser(c *gin.Context) {
	// Anti-pattern: route param is "user_id" but DTO field is "UserID"
	userID := c.Param("user_id")

	var dto UpdateUserDTO
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&dto); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: manual mapping required because names drift
	dto.UserID = userID

	// Anti-pattern: another route uses "id" but DTO expects "UserID"
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
		"user_id":    dto.UserID,
		"user_name":  dto.UserName,
		"email":      dto.Email,
	})
}

func HandleGetUser(c *gin.Context) {
	// Anti-pattern: this handler uses "id" as param name
	id := c.Param("id")

	// Anti-pattern: but DTO still expects "UserID"
	dto := UpdateUserDTO{
		UserID: id,
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
		"user_id": dto.UserID,
	})
}
